spec: SUFFICIENCY AUDIT + adversarial stress test: conditional Flow-Matching CNF vs vanilla and strengthened classical count baselines
score_difference_direction: FM minus comparator; negative means FM has lower/better proper score.

classical_baselines.note: CQR NLL is based on an interpolated quantile PMF, so count-likelihood interpretation should focus on vs_best_count_classical; CRPS is fair for all PMFs.

crps_representation.nb_zinb_mixture_flexible_nb: analytic discrete count PMF on support 0..support_max
crps_representation.cqr: interpolated quantile discrete count PMF on support 0..support_max
crps_representation.flow_matching: continuous dequantized CNF sampled, rescaled, rounded to nearest count, histogrammed on support 0..support_max

flow_matching.likelihood: exact 1-D CNF density integrated over the observed count bin by Gauss-Legendre quadrature

tost.bootstrap: patient-clustered for Brigham; row-clustered for Mendeley; split-prefixed clusters for pooled repeated-split summary

stress_test_summary.one_line: The vanilla-NB CRPS win does not survive the best-classical stress test; do not claim a generative distributional win.

fair_discrete_crps_vs_vanilla_nb.interpretation: CRPS is computed for all models on discrete count PMFs; FM PMF is rounded-sample count mass.

stronger_count_baselines_zinb_mixture_fl.comparison: FM minus best of NB, ZINB, two-component NB mixture, flexible-mean NB.

best_classical_including_cqr.comparison: FM minus best of count-classical baselines plus CQR split-conformal PMF.

patient_level_leakage.interpretation: Brigham split is by Patient_Code groups, not by rows.

ten_split_stability.required: CRPS SUPERIOR in all 10 splits for every cohort versus best classical.

one_line_conclusion: The vanilla-NB CRPS win does not survive the best-classical stress test; do not claim a generative distributional win.
